what: Goal: minimize API calls and context bloat
title: Claude Code working rules (fishr)
text: Prefer ONE request per phase: Design → Implement → Fix failures → Polish. iterate tiny steps. Batch edits run checks per phase.

title: Claude Code working rules (fishr)
text: NEVER read whole files default. First: use rg locate symbols. read smallest necessary window (≈ ±80–150 lines). context needed, ask explicitly section expand.

title: Claude Code working rules (fishr)
text: paste full R CMD check, devtools::check(), benchmark output conversation. ERROR lines WARNING lines immediate stack/trace context (≤ 60 lines total) benchmarks, report summary table / key numbers.

title: Claude Code working rules (fishr)
text: editing, prefer single coherent patch addresses items phase. large edits, provide short “changed” list + exact files touched.

title: Claude Code working rules (fishr)
text: Default: write tests can run offline possible test require login/network, ensure guarded skip_if().

title: Claude Code working rules (fishr)
text: making edits non-trivial refactor, start producing: 1) design sketch 2) cache semantics & invalidation plan 3) performance risks 4) concurrency/locking assumptions 5) test plan wait confirmation implement.

title: Claude Code working rules (fishr)
text: Always ask pushing master Feature branches : new functions, refactors, bug fixes touching multiple files, cache/logic changes, non-trivial code Direct master : typo fixes, small doc tweaks, version bumps, NEWS, release prep committing: pause say “Changes ready review” user can check RStudio Git pane Wait go-ahead committing

title: Claude Code working rules (fishr)
text: internal/non-exported functions, use @noRd roxygen comments

title: Claude Code working rules (fishr)
text: completing subtask, run /compact. unrelated tasks, run /clear.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Gregory Jefferis. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Jefferis G (2026). fishr: Access Fish2 FlyEM Dataset. R package version 0.1.0, https://flyconnectome.github.io/fishr/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {fishr: Access the Fish2 FlyEM Dataset},   author = {Gregory Jefferis},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://flyconnectome.github.io/fishr/}, }

title: Access the Fish2 FlyEM Dataset
text: goal fishr provide natverse access fish2 larval zebrafish whole-brain EM dataset. follows general pattern malecns malevnc, providing dataset-specific helpers top neuprintr package bridge broader natverse connectomics tooling computational neuroanatomy. fish2 dataset currently proofread Janelia Research Campus part multi-institution collaboration including Harvard Google Research. initial package focuses : dataset selection helpers (choose_fish(), with_fish()) body id lookup (fish_ids()) neuprint metadata connectivity queries (fish_neuprint_meta(), fish_connection_table()) DVID body annotations (fish_dvid_annotations()) neuroglancer meshes skeletons (read_fish_meshes(), read_fish_neurons()) support coconat interface (register_fish_coconat())

title: Access the Fish2 FlyEM Dataset
text: new R, recommend installing RStudio IDE base R detailed https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/#download. install fishr package dependencies, R terminal : also need make sure authenticated clio neuprint.
code: # install natmanager if needed if (!requireNamespace("natmanager")) install.packages("natmanager") natmanager::install(pkgs = "fishr")

title: Access the Fish2 FlyEM Dataset
text: Access neuprint requires authentication. recommend running: interactive R session following prompts. help record token offer set fish2 dataset/server default neuprint commands. See https://github.com/natverse/neuprintr#authentication learn relevant environment variables. need authenticate Clio API interact Clio/DVID annotation system. via Google OAuth “dance” web browser automatically triggered choose_fish() functions require Clio access. Note Clio neuprint tokens look similar, . Note also neuprint token appears indefinite Clio token currently lasts 3 weeks.
code: library(fishr) fish_setup()

title: Access the Fish2 FlyEM Dataset
text: example shows basic activities including finding ids type, fetching metadata connectivity information, reading plotting retinal ganglion cell meshes.
code: library(fishr) library(nat) # you only have to run this once, but you can  fish_setup()  # find some RGCs rgc10=fish_ids("RGC")[1:10] # get metadata fish_neuprint_meta(rgc10)  # connectivity query fish_connection_table(rgc10, partners = "outputs")  # fetch some RGC meshes rgcm <- read_fish_meshes(rgc10) plot3d(rgcm)  # read and plot one fish skeleton rgcn <- read_fish_neurons(rgc10[1]) plot3d(rgcn, col = "cyan")  # Get meshes for some ROIs. NB not all ROIs have meshes. roi.meshes <- fish_roi_meshes(c("Midbrain", "Hindbrain"), .progress = "none") wire3d(roi.meshes, col='grey')

title: Access the Fish2 FlyEM Dataset
text: fishr set core functions lot useful functionality R packages interacting FlyEM connectome data ecosystem, especially malevnc neuprintr.

title: Access the Fish2 FlyEM Dataset
text: malevnc especially useful interacting annotation segmentation infrastructure (clio/DVID). can use function malevnc package wrapping with_fish(). ever use fish dataset choose_fish() per session means subsequent malevnc functions target fish2 dataset. already happens follow default prompts run fish_setup(). key end user application malevnc package right now probably setting clio annotations via malevnc::manc_annotate_body()
code: with_fish(malevnc::clio_fields()) choose_fish() malevnc::clio_fields()

title: Access the Fish2 FlyEM Dataset
text: Similarly can use functions base neuprintr package. followed default prompts fish_setup() using FlyEM datasets, fish2 default . use neuprint datasets neuprintr uses last () neuprint connection made session target dataset.
code: fc=fish_neuprint() fc neuprintr::neuprint_ROIs(conn = fc)

title: Access the Fish2 FlyEM Dataset
text: can also register fish2 extra dataset coconatfly. Even fish2 fly package provides uniform approach connectome analysis including connectivity clustering functions. fetching metadata can try connectivity clustering subset neurons, case right eye …
code: library(dplyr) register_fish_coconat() rgcmeta=coconatfly::cf_meta(coconatfly::cf_ids(fish2 = "RGC")) rgcmeta rgcmeta %>%    filter(side=="R") %>%    # nb we need group = F because the partner neurons aren't really typed yet   coconatfly::cf_cosine_plot(partners = 'o', group = FALSE)  hc = rgcmeta %>%    filter(side=="R") %>%    # nb we need group = F because the partner neurons aren't really typed yet   coconatfly::cf_cosine_plot(partners = 'o', group = FALSE, heatmap=F) hcm = coconat::add_cluster_info(cf_meta(hc), hc, h = 1)

what: Connectivity query for fish2 neurons — fish_connection_table
title: Connectivity query for fish2 neurons — fish_connection_table
text: Connectivity query fish2 neurons

title: Connectivity query for fish2 neurons — fish_connection_table
code: fish_connection_table(   ids,   partners = c("inputs", "outputs"),   moredetails = FALSE,   details = TRUE,   summary = FALSE,   threshold = 1L,   roi = NULL,   by.roi = FALSE,   conn = NULL,   ...,   dataset = fish_default_dataset() )

title: Connectivity query for fish2 neurons — fish_connection_table
text: ids set body ids (see fish_ids range ways specify ). partners Either inputs outputs. Redundant prepost, probably clearer. moredetails FALSE (default) use columns returned neuprint_connection_table, TRUE join additional fields fish_neuprint_meta, character vector naming specific extra fields (e.g. c("group")). details Whether request detailed metadata columns directly neuprint_connection_table (default TRUE). summary TRUE one query neuron given, summarises connectivity grouped partner. threshold return partners >= integer value. Default 1 returns partners. threshold applied ROI weight roi argument specified, otherwise whole neuron. roi single ROI. Use neuprint_ROIs see available. .roi logical, whether break neurons' connectivity region interest (ROI) conn Optional, neuprint_connection object. Defaults fish_neuprint ensure query targets fish2. ... Additional arguments passed neuprint_connection_table. dataset optional, dataset want query. NULL, default specified R environ file used , failing current connection, used. See neuprint_login details.

title: Connectivity query for fish2 neurons — fish_connection_table
text: data.frame.

title: Connectivity query for fish2 neurons — fish_connection_table

what: Read DVID body annotations for fish2 body ids — fish_dvid_annotations
title: Read DVID body annotations for fish2 body ids — fish_dvid_annotations
text: thin wrapper around malevnc::manc_dvid_annotations   targeting fish2 dataset.  Supports /field:regex query strings   filter annotations locally (see Details).

title: Read DVID body annotations for fish2 body ids — fish_dvid_annotations
code: fish_dvid_annotations(   ids = NULL,   node = "neutu",   rval = c("data.frame", "list"),   columns_show = NULL,   cache = FALSE )

title: Read DVID body annotations for fish2 body ids — fish_dvid_annotations
text: ids One body ids, NULL (default) fetch annotations, query string (see Details). node DVID node (UUID) query. default value 'neutu' uses active neutu node .e. normally date. rval Whether return fully parsed "data.frame" (default) R "list". columns_show Whether show columns '_user' '_time' suffix. Accepted values: 'user', 'time', ''. cache Whether cache result 5 minutes (default FALSE).

title: Read DVID body annotations for fish2 body ids — fish_dvid_annotations
text: tibble body annotations. See   malevnc::manc_dvid_annotations column details.

title: Read DVID body annotations for fish2 body ids — fish_dvid_annotations
text: Query string formats filtering DVID annotations: "/type:RGC..*" Match type field regex     RGC..*. "RGC.*" Equivalent shorthand — bare strings default     type field. "/status:Traced" Match different field. Regex queries automatically anchored (^...$) unless pattern already starts ^.  Queries fetch annotations (cache=TRUE) filter locally. neuprint-based id lookups, use fish_ids instead.

title: Read DVID body annotations for fish2 body ids — fish_dvid_annotations
code: # \donttest{ # fetch annotations for specific bodies fish_dvid_annotations(c(100003384, 100003412)) #> # A tibble: 2 × 11 #>     bodyid connectivity_type per_node_sc status user  group instance type  class #>      <dbl> <chr>                   <dbl> <chr>  <chr> <int> <chr>    <chr> <chr> #> 1   1.00e8 NA                         NA Senso… NA        0 RGC_R    RGC   NA    #> 2   1.00e8 NA                         NA Senso… bergs     0 RGC_R    RGC   NA    #> # ℹ 2 more variables: keywords <chr>, comment <chr> # } if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # fetch all annotations using 5m cache if possible df <- fish_dvid_annotations(cache=TRUE)  # filter by type regex df <- fish_dvid_annotations("/type:RGC.*", cache=T) } # } # \donttest{ # shorthand for type field df <- fish_dvid_annotations("RGC", cache=T) df #> # A tibble: 4,030 × 11 #>     bodyid connectivity_type per_node_sc status user  group instance type  class #>      <dbl> <chr>                   <dbl> <chr>  <chr> <int> <chr>    <chr> <chr> #>  1  1.00e8 NA                         NA Senso… NA        0 RGC_R    RGC   NA    #>  2  1.00e8 NA                         NA Senso… bergs     0 RGC_R    RGC   NA    #>  3  1.00e8 NA                         NA Senso… NA       NA RGC_R    RGC   NA    #>  4  1.00e8 NA                         NA Senso… bergs     0 RGC_R    RGC   NA    #>  5  1.00e8 NA                         NA Senso… NA        0 RGC_R    RGC   NA    #>  6  1.00e8 NA                         NA Senso… NA        0 RGC_R    RGC   NA    #>  7  1.00e8 NA                         NA Senso… NA        0 RGC_R    RGC   NA    #>  8  1.00e8 NA                         NA Senso… NA        0 RGC_R    RGC   NA    #>  9  1.00e8 NA                         NA Senso… NA        0 RGC_R    RGC   NA    #> 10  1.00e8 NA                         NA Senso… NA        0 RGC_R    RGC   NA    #> # ℹ 4,020 more rows #> # ℹ 2 more variables: keywords <chr>, comment <chr> # }

what: Resolve fish2 body ids from a variety of inputs — fish_ids
title: Resolve fish2 body ids from a variety of inputs — fish_ids
text: Extracts body ids vectors, data.frames, query strings.   Query strings passed malevnc::manc_ids via   fish2 neuprint server.  filtering DVID annotations field, see   fish_dvid_annotations.

title: Resolve fish2 body ids from a variety of inputs — fish_ids
code: fish_ids(   x,   mustWork = TRUE,   as_character = TRUE,   integer64 = FALSE,   unique = TRUE,   conn = NULL )

title: Resolve fish2 body ids from a variety of inputs — fish_ids
text: x numeric/character vector body ids, data.frame bodyid column, neuprint query string (e.g. "DNa02", ":status='Traced'"). mustWork TRUE (default) error raised ids found. as_character Whether return ids character (default TRUE). Character safest default body ids may exceed 53-bit integer limit double-precision floating point (2^53 - 1). integer64 Whether return ids bit64::integer64 (default FALSE). Takes precedence as_character. unique Whether de-duplicate result (default TRUE). conn neuprint_connection object (default NULL uses fish_neuprint).

title: Resolve fish2 body ids from a variety of inputs — fish_ids
text: character vector body ids (default),   bit64::integer64 integer64=TRUE   as_character=FALSE.

title: Resolve fish2 body ids from a variety of inputs — fish_ids

title: Login to fish2 neuprint server — fish_neuprint
code: fish_neuprint(   token = fish_neuprint_token(),   dataset = NULL,   Force = FALSE,   use_clio = FALSE,   ... )

title: Login to fish2 neuprint server — fish_neuprint
text: token neuprint authorisation token obtained e.g. neuprint.janelia.org website. dataset Allows override neuprint dataset fish_neuprint (otherwise chosen based value options(fishr.dataset), normally changed choose_fish_dataset). Force Passed neuprintr::neuprint_login. use_clio Whether use live Clio lookup fish dataset settings opening neuprint connection. default FALSE reuses cached live lookup results available otherwise relies built-fish2 neuprint settings. ... Additional arguments passed neuprint_login.

title: Login to fish2 neuprint server — fish_neuprint
text: neuprint_connection object returned   neuprint_login.

title: Login to fish2 neuprint server — fish_neuprint
text: possible use token across public   private neuprint servers using email address. However   seem work users. giving , try   using recently issued token private server rather   older tokens. need pass specific token can use   token argument, also setting Force=TRUE ensure   specified token used already tried log   current session.

title: Login to fish2 neuprint server — fish_neuprint
code: # \donttest{ conn <- fish_neuprint() conn #> Connection to neuPrint server: #>   https://neuprint-fish2.janelia.org #> with default dataset: #>    fish2  #> Login active since: Thu, 16 Apr 2026 20:20:23 GMT # }

what: fishr: Access the Fish2 FlyEM Dataset — fishr-package
title: fishr: Access the Fish2 FlyEM Dataset — fishr-package
text: thin wrapper around malevnc package providing access fish2 larval zebrafish EM dataset proofread Janelia Research Campus part collaboration Engert / Lichtman labs Harvard Google Research. Currently provides convenience functions reading meshes setting dataset options.

title: fishr: Access the Fish2 FlyEM Dataset — fishr-package
text: fishr.dataset name active fish2 dataset (default   "fish2"). Set choose_fish_dataset. following malevnc.* options set choose_fish   with_fish point malevnc package fish2   servers:  malevnc.server DVID server URL (set via Clio lookup). malevnc.rootnode DVID root node UUID (set via Clio lookup). malevnc.neuprint neuprint server URL (fallback:   https://neuprint-fish2.janelia.org). malevnc.neuprint_dataset neuprint dataset name (fallback:   "fish2").

title: fishr: Access the Fish2 FlyEM Dataset — fishr-package
text: Maintainer: Gregory Jefferis jefferis@gmail.com (ORCID)

title: fishr: Access the Fish2 FlyEM Dataset — fishr-package
code: # \donttest{ options()[grepl("^fishr", names(options()))] #> $fishr.dataset #> [1] "fish2" #>  #> $fishr.dataset_options #> $fishr.dataset_options$fish2 #> $fishr.dataset_options$fish2$malevnc.server #> [1] "https://fishemf-cleaving.janelia.org" #>  #> $fishr.dataset_options$fish2$malevnc.rootnode #> [1] "634eed0328dc4fe183f2bcb9306572db" #>  #> $fishr.dataset_options$fish2$malevnc.dataset #> [1] "fish2" #>  #> $fishr.dataset_options$fish2$malevnc.neuprint #> [1] "https://neuprint-fish2.janelia.org" #>  #> $fishr.dataset_options$fish2$malevnc.neuprint_dataset #> [1] "fish2" #>  #>  #>  # } if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ with_fish(read_fish_meshes(12345)) } # }

Alternatively must multiply point locations   neuroglancer c(16,16,15) obtain points nm space.

what: Evaluate an expression after temporarily setting malevnc options — with_fish
title: Evaluate an expression after temporarily setting malevnc options — with_fish
text: with_fish temporarily changes server/dataset options   malevnc package target fish2 dataset   running expression. choose_fish_dataset sets default dataset used   fish_* functions. recommended way access fish2 dataset   variants. Unlike choose_fish permanently change   default dataset used callers use functions malevnc   package directly. choose_fish swaps default malevnc   dataset fish2 dataset. means functions   malevnc package target fish2 instead. recommended   use with_fish temporarily unless   intention using FlyEM datasets. switch default fishr   dataset please see choose_fish_dataset. Clio dataset lookup unavailable, choose_fish falls back   built-neuprint-configuration main fish2 dataset.   Clio-backed functionality may unavailable   session.

title: Evaluate an expression after temporarily setting malevnc options — with_fish

title: Evaluate an expression after temporarily setting malevnc options — with_fish
text: expr expression involving malevnc/fishr functions evaluate specified dataset. dataset name dataset reported Clio (default "fish2"). set Whether set relevant package options just return list required options. use_clio Whether use live Clio lookup fish dataset settings. default NA first reuses cached live lookup dataset, otherwise tries Clio falls back baked-fish2 neuprint settings available. Use FALSE avoid Clio rely cached built-settings. Use TRUE require live Clio lookup.
